extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won the sprint finish, while VAN AERT Wout took yellow despite finishing second by capitalizing on time bonuses and flat roads protecting GC riders.
tactical_patterns: CORT Magnus swept all three 1-point KOM sprints from early breakaway, showing how low-category parcours creates polka-dot opportunities.
Headwind conditions neutralized expected echelon chaos on coastal roads, protecting GC riders on supposedly dangerous narrow Danish stage.
B&B Hotels riders deliberately dropped back after first KOM to cede points to CORT Magnus, revealing pre-race jersey negotiation.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: 11th win of season and first Tour stage victory since returning from life-threatening 2020 injury
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: took yellow jersey despite second place finish, demonstrating time bonus accumulation strategy across flat stages
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: second consecutive runner-up finish in two days, showing sprint speed deficit to top-tier finishers
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: soloed KOM classification by being only Danish rider in break on home roads
rider_signals.ROLLAND Pierre: neutral: most experienced rider in race with 13th Tour start, used as B&B Hotels KOM decoy
